military.title: The Bunker
military.description: Deep beneath the palace, the generals have shown you their greatest secret. A command console. One button. One red button. Whatever happens next, there is no going back.
military.outcome_good: You press the button. A blinding flash illuminates the horizon. Hours later, the reports come in — the entire population has perished. Your enemies, your allies, everyone. You sit alone on a throne of ash. Emperor of the Wasteland.
military.outcome_bad: You press the button. The missiles fly — but not toward your own people. The neighboring country is annihilated. The military erupts in celebration. You drink with them through the night. You never see the blade that finds your throat.

business.title: The Vault
business.description: The elite have offered you an exit. A private vault, filled with untraceable accounts. A jet waiting on the tarmac with engines running. You could disappear tonight.
business.outcome_good: You seize the accounts and vanish. The jet is waiting, the beach house keys already in your pocket. You spend your final years in luxury, untouchable and forgotten. It was a good run.
business.outcome_bad: You reach for the accounts. At the airport, uniformed men step forward. The money is counterfeit — a trap laid by the very people you trusted. You are taken to a cell. The official report says suicide.

people.title: The Zen Garden
people.description: The people have built you this. Flowers, stone paths, absolute silence. A panel on the wall bears a single instruction: let go. For the first time in years, no one is watching.
people.outcome_good: You sit in the garden and, for the first time, let your guard down. Something has changed. You call for free elections. The people flood the streets. You watch from a window, surprised to feel relief. You live quietly for decades, tending your garden, until you die peacefully at ninety-four.
people.outcome_bad: You close your eyes in the garden, finally at ease. The jasmine is beautiful. You never hear him coming. An anarchist steps from between the flowers. Your last thought is the smell of jasmine.
